entries.title: Poke makes using AI agents as easy as sending a text
entries.description: Poke brings AI agents to everyday users via text message by handling tasks and automations without complex setup, apps, or technical know-how.

entries.title: AWS boss explains why investing billions in both Anthropic and OpenAI is an OK conflict
entries.description: AWS has an ingrained culture of handling competition, he explained, because the cloud giant also competes with its partners.

entries.title: Tubi is the first streamer to launch a native app within ChatGPT
entries.description: Tubi becomes the first streaming service to offer an app integration within ChatGPT, the AI chatbot that millions of users turn to for answers.

entries.title: Astropad’s Workbench reimagines remote desktop for AI agents, not IT support
entries.description: Astropad’s Workbench lets users remotely monitor and control AI agents on Mac Minis from iPhone or iPad, with low-latency streaming and mobile access.

entries.title: ChatGPT finally offers $100/month Pro plan
entries.description: OpenAI announced on Thursday something that power users have been asking for: a $100/month plan. Previously, subscriptions jumped from $20 to $200 per month.

entries.title: Florida AG announces investigation into OpenAI over shooting that allegedly involved ChatGPT

entries.title: After data breach, $10B-valued startup Mercor is having a month
entries.description: After falling victim to a hacker, Mercor is facing lawsuits and reportedly losing big-name customers.

entries.title: Meta AI app climbs to No. 5 on the App Store after Muse Spark launch
entries.description: The app was ranking No. 57 on the App Store just before Meta AI's new model launched. Now it's No. 5 — and rising.

entries.title: Is Anthropic limiting the release of Mythos to protect the internet — or Anthropic?

entries.title: Google and Intel deepen AI infrastructure partnership
entries.description: The two tech giants are looking to co-develop custom chips, at a time when demand for CPUs is high due to a growing global shortage.

entries.title: Sierra’s Bret Taylor says the era of clicking buttons is over

entries.title: Amazon CEO takes aim at Nvidia, Intel, Starlink, more in annual shareholder letter
entries.description: Andy Jassy's annual shareholder letter reads something like a diss track to a wide range of competitors as he defends spending $200 billion in capex.

entries.title: Final 2 days to save up to $500 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 ticket
entries.description: Ticket discounts of up to $500 will end tomorrow, April 10, at 11:59 p.m. PT. After that, prices for TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 go up again. Miss this, and you’ll be paying more for the same access to on

entries.title: Sam Altman responds to ‘incendiary’ New Yorker article after attack on his home
entries.description: The OpenAI CEO's new blog post responds to both an apparent attack on his home and an in-depth New Yorker profile raising questions about his trustworthiness.

entries.title: Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw’s creator from accessing Claude
entries.description: This ban took place after Claude's pricing changed for OpenClaw users last week.

entries.title: Stalking victim sues OpenAI, claims ChatGPT fueled her abuser’s delusions and ignored her warnings
entries.description: OpenAI ignored three warnings that a ChatGPT user was dangerous — including its own mass-casualty flag — while he stalked and harassed his ex-girlfriend, a new lawsuit alleges.

entries.title: TechCrunch is heading to Tokyo — and bringing the Startup Battlefield with it

entries.title: Last 24 hours: Save up to $500 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass
entries.description: This is it. Tonight is your last chance to lock in savings of up to $500 for your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass. These discounts end at 11:59 p.m. PT.
